channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Early favorite emerges in Republican primary for NJ governor: poll
item.description: NEW JERSEY (PIX11) – A candidate has emerged as an early favorite in the Republican primary for New Jersey governor, a new Emerson College Polling/PIX11/The Hill poll shows.
item.dc:creator: Finn Hoogensen, PIX 11

item.title: Trump suggests it was a mistake Biden didn't pardon himself
item.description: President Trump on Wednesday repeatedly suggested it was a mistake that former President Biden did not preemptively pardon himself before leaving office. Trump, in a sit-down interview with Fox News host Sean Hannity, also danced around questions on whether Biden should be investigated.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Cracks emerge in House GOP after speaker's threat to saddle California wildfire aid with conditions
item.description: Some U.S. House Republicans in California are breaking with Speaker Mike Johnson over his suggestion that federal disaster aid be coupled with as-yet unspecified conditions on the wildfire-ravaged state.
item.dc:creator: Michael R. Blood And Kevin Freking, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump taps Sean Curran as next Secret Service director
item.description: Curran was part of Trump's security details during an attempted assassination in Butler, Pennsylvania, last July.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: Trump says he may withhold federal aid for Los Angeles if California doesn't change water policies
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump on Wednesday threatened to withhold federal disaster aid for wildfire-ravaged Los Angeles unless California leaders change the state's approach on its management of water.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO and CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Department of Justice freezes all civil rights division cases: report
item.description: The Department of Justice sent a memo to the interim director of the civil rights division, ordering a freeze to all ongoing litigation and a stop to any new cases.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How Trump's gender order impacts Californians' passports, driver's licenses
item.description: President Donald Trump's executive order declaring the U.S. only recognizes two genders, male and female, has raised questions about what this means for the validity of documents distributed to transgender Californians with their preferred gender marker.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, Fox 5

item.title: Episcopal bishop says she'll continue to pray for Trump, who lashed out at her over viral sermon
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Right Rev. Mariann Edgar Budde, bishop of the Diocese of Washington, said Wednesday that she will continue to pray for President Donald Trump, hours after he lashed out at her over a sermon. “I don’t consider him an enemy,” Budde told The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: TIFFANY STANLEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pentagon is sending 1,500 active duty troops to help secure the US-Mexico border
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Pentagon said Wednesday it has begun deploying 1,500 active duty troops to help secure the southern border, putting in motion plans President Donald Trump laid out in executive orders shortly after he took office to crack down on immigration.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR and TARA COPP, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Democratic infighting is rising as Trump takes center stage
item.description: Democrats are finding themselves mired in infighting and schoolyard sniping just as President Trump begins his new term. Former first lady Jill Biden and Rep. Nancy Pelosi’s (D-Calif.
item.dc:creator: Amie Parnes, NewsNation

item.title: The Hitchhiker’s Guide to a probable weekend session to confirm Trump nominees
item.description: We're approaching the first weekend of President Donald Trump's second term – and the Senate is already running behind in confirming his Cabinet nominees.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump highlights partnership investing $500 billion in AI
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump on Tuesday talked up a joint venture investing up to $500 billion for infrastructure tied to artificial intelligence by a new partnership formed by OpenAI, Oracle and SoftBank.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK and ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump administration cancels travel for refugees already cleared to come to America
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Refugees who had been approved to come to the United States before a deadline next week suspending America’s refugee resettlement program have had their travel plans canceled by the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: FARNOUSH AMIRI, REBECCA SANTANA and GISELA SALOMON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Meta denies platforms are forcing users to follow Trump, Vance
item.description: "Those accounts are managed by the White House so with a new administration, the content on those Pages changes," Andy Stone wrote.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's Day One actions reversed Biden-era health policies, including efforts to expand ObamaCare
item.description: President Trump is rolling back Biden healthcare policies, such as expansions to the Affordable Care Act – a move Democrats described as an "attack" on the federal program.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump’s threat of tariffs and sanctions on Russia over Ukraine may fall flat
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump threatened on Wednesday to impose stiff taxes, tariffs and sanctions on Russia if an agreement isn’t reached to end the war in Ukraine, a warning that is likely fall on deaf ears in the Kremlin.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rep. Nancy Mace says she is considering a run for South Carolina governor
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Republican Rep. Nancy Mace of South Carolina said Wednesday that she is “seriously considering” a gubernatorial run in 2026 and will make a final decision in the coming weeks.
item.dc:creator: MEG KINNARD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Man who got Jan. 6 pardon is arrested on federal gun charge
item.description: The Florida man appears to be the first Jan. 6 rioter to face additional legal trouble after the sweeping clemency bestowed on those who rioted at the Capitol.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Everything Trump did in the first executive orders and actions of his presidency
item.description: President Donald Trump began his second administration with a blitz of policy actions to reorient U.S. government priorities.
item.dc:creator: Bill Barrow, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: GOP congressman wants bishop 'added to deportation list' after Trump prayer service
item.description: Rep. Mike Collins heavily criticized statements made by the Right Rev. Mariann Budde on Tuesday at the inaugural prayer service held for President Trump.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, Fox 5

item.title: Pete Hegseth’s former sister-in-law alleges abuse against second wife in affidavit to Senate
item.description: Senators vetting the nomination of Minnesotan Pete Hegseth for defense secretary received an affidavit Tuesday from a former sister-in-law alleging that the onetime Fox News host was abusive to his second wife, to the point where she feared for her safety. Hegseth denies the allegations.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump open to Elon Musk buying TikTok
item.description: TikTok restored service to U.S. users Sunday afternoon and credited Trump for pushing to keep the app operational.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: Trump set to visit NC, California, Nevada
item.description: The president said he will be going Friday to North Carolina, which he said “has been abandoned by the Democrats.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: New York plan would give money to new moms: Who qualifies
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – New York would give hundreds of dollars in financial support to low-income parents under a new proposal. Last week, Gov. Kathy Hochul announced a proposal to give low-income parents a “birth allowance” during pregnancy and at the birth of their child.
item.dc:creator: Finn Hoogensen, PIX 11

item.title: Refugees set to resettle in Minnesota next month now barred entry after Trump order
item.description: Jane Graupman, the executive director of International Institute of Minnesota, and Nasreen Sajady, the executive director of the Afghan Cultural Society, joined Minnesota Now to talk about the impacts of Trump’s order to temporarily suspend all refugee resettlement.
item.dc:creator: Emily Bright and Aleesa Kuznetsov, MPR News

item.title: NJ governor sends letter to Trump seeking to end congestion pricing
item.description: NEW JERSEY (PIX11) – New Jersey Gov. Phil Murphy sent a letter to President Donald Trump on Monday seeking to end congestion pricing in New York City. Congestion pricing went into effect on Jan. 5.
item.dc:creator: Finn Hoogensen, PIX 11

item.title: Michelle Obama not attending Donald Trump's inauguration: officials
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – The former first lady of the U.S., Michelle Obama, isn't attending President-elect Donald Trump's inauguration on Monday, according to her office. It's not clear why she's not at the swearing-in ceremony. Her husband, former President Barack Obama, is taking part in the event.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Trump sworn in as 47th president, celebrates with supporters
item.description: On a frigid holiday Monday in Washington, D.C., Donald Trump took the oath of office for a second time to become the 47th president of the United States.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, PIX 11

item.title: Live updates: Trump inauguration weekend commences as protesters rally in Washington
item.description: Inauguration festivities will begin in full force Saturday as President-elect Trump and his allies — and protesters — arrive in Washington ahead of Monday's inauguration of the 47th president of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: The Hill Staff, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Special session rebuttal on illegal immigration continues in Florida
item.description: The governor gave his order Monday with House and Senate leadership pushing back, and that's not the only rebuttal we've seen in recent days.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC
